response: The internet originated from 1960s U.S. Department of Defense computer networking research, with its precursor ARPANET first demonstrated in 1969, before the 1983 transition to TCP/IP protocols created the modern internet's technical foundation. Subsequent landmark developments included Tim Berners-Lee's 1989 invention of the World Wide Web, the 1993 Mosaic browser that brought the web to general users, the late 1990s dot-com boom and 2001 crash, and the later emergence of social media, cloud computing, and widespread mobile internet after the 2007 iPhone launch. Today, the internet connects more than 5 billion people globally and forms the underlying infrastructure for nearly all areas of modern daily life.
